lines.en_lit: but from this side or from that side say no, necessary
Rabbi Elazar said in the name of bar Kappara: Rabbi Meir would say: limbs of a burnt-offering that remained one should make a separate arrangement for them and arrange them And even on Shabbat.
What is teaching us we learned On every day, there were four arrangements there, Rabbi Avin said: is needed only disqualified
But specifically the fire has taken hold of them But the fire taken hold of them, no,
There are who say Both valid and disqualified if the fire has taken hold of them yes, but if no,
And even on Shabbat. we learned But this day, five.

Rav Aḥa bar Ya’akov said: is necessary, it could enter your mind to say this applies when Yom Kippur occurs after Shabbat, that fats of Shabbat are sacrificed on Yom Kippur. However, in the middle of the week, no, teaches us
Rava said: Who is this that does not care about his flour, Did we learn On every day. difficult.
disagrees with Rav Huna, who said: The beginning overrides the end does not override
the itself: Rav Huna said: The beginning daily offering overrides the end does not override
What does not override Rav Ḥisda said: does override Shabbat, it does not override ritual impurity. And Rabba said: overrides ritual impurity, but it does not override Shabbat.

Abaye said to It a difficulty to your and it a difficulty to of Rav Ḥisda. It difficult to your opinion What is different ritual impurity Because it is written “in its season” in ritual impurity. also Shabbat. “in its season” even on Shabbat.
And it difficult to Rav Ḥisda: What is different Shabbat Because it is written “in its season,” even on Shabbat. also ritual impurity. “in its season” even in ritual impurity.
said to him: It not difficult to my and it does not a difficulty to Rav Ḥisda. It not difficult to my the end of is like its beginning.
